old: 空气污染危害人的健康。
new: 他的行为危害了社会。
reason: Nearly identical to sentence_1 (both about pollution harming health); duplicate meaning within the same entry

new: His behavior jeopardized society.
reason: Updated to match corrected sentence_2

old: 他的行为违反了基本的好人标准。
new: 他的行为违反了基本道德标准。
reason: 好人标准 ('standards of being a good person') is an unnatural collocation for 违反; 道德标准 (moral standards) is the natural collocation

old: His behavior goes against basic standards of being a good person.
new: His behavior goes against basic moral standards.
reason: Updated to match corrected sentence_1

old: 大家把城市团团围住了。
new: 士兵们把敌军团团围住了。
reason: 'Everyone encircled the city' is semantically odd — cities are not typically encircled by 大家; soldiers surrounding enemy troops is a natural context for 围

new: The soldiers completely encircled the enemy troops.
reason: Updated to match corrected sentence_1

new: magnificent
reason: 伟大 means great/magnificent, not 'mighty' (which implies physical power); the sentences illustrate greatness/magnificence, not physical might

old: This elder was mighty in history.
new: This elder was magnificent in history.
reason: Updated to match corrected definition

new: He possesses great strength.
reason: Updated to match corrected definition

old: This nation has a mighty spirit.
new: This nation has a magnificent spirit.
reason: Updated to match corrected definition

new: 我的胃不舒服，什么都不想吃。
reason: '我的胃有点饿了' is unnatural Chinese — you say 我有点饿了, not attribute hunger to the stomach organ specifically

old: My stomach is a little hungry.
new: My stomach feels uncomfortable and I don't want to eat anything.
reason: Updated to match corrected sentence_3

new: 他用力握住了铁棒。
reason: Sentence uses 把握 (bǎwò) not the headword 握; also duplicates the 'opportunity' theme of sentence_1

old: You must grasp every opportunity well.
new: He gripped the iron bar tightly.
reason: Updated to match corrected sentence_3

old: He is very active on the political arena.
new: He is very active in the political arena.
reason: Preposition error: 'in the arena', not 'on the arena'

old: 这些物品从国外进口的。
new: 这些物品是从国外进口的。
reason: Missing 是 — the sentence requires the copula 是 for grammatical correctness

new: Water is an important substance.
reason: 'Matter' used as a count noun ('an important matter') sounds like 'issue/affair' in English; 'substance' correctly renders 物质 in this physical science context

new: 工作压力和健康是相关的。
reason: Sentence_3 is nearly identical to sentence_1 ('这两个问题是相关的' vs '这两件事是相关的'), providing no new illustration of the word

new: Work stress and health are related.
reason: Updated to match corrected sentence_3

new: small-sized
reason: 小型 means 'small in scale/size', not 'miniature' (which implies a scaled-down model replica); 小型 is used for small cars, companies, cameras — small-sized is more accurate

new: 他买了一台小型打印机。
reason: 小型 does not collocate naturally with the vague 东西; it modifies specific object types like equipment or vehicles

old: He likes to collect small things.
new: He bought a small-sized printer.
reason: Updated to match corrected sentence_3

new: 她的写作风格很独特。
reason: Headword 写作 is absent from sentence_3; sentence should illustrate the word being studied

old: Her dream is to become a writer.
new: Her writing style is very unique.
reason: Updated to match corrected sentence_3

new: We enjoyed a concert.
reason: 'Appreciated a concert' is awkward English; 欣赏 a performance means to enjoy/watch it with appreciation, rendered naturally as 'enjoyed'
